channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Documentary explores a sperm donor’s journey to meet his biological children across Canada
item.description: A new Canadian documentary set to premiere this week tells the extraordinary story of a sperm donor who reconnects with dozens of his biological children. The film follows the man as he meets around 6...

item.title: Man survives cardiac arrest thanks to public defibrillator and passer-by assistance
item.description: Spencer Stevenson, a 54-year-old commuter, suffered a sudden cardiac arrest while on his way to work in London in December. As he reached the top of the stairs at Liverpool Street station, he felt diz...

item.title: Critique of DEI Policies and Their Impact on Individual Merit and Economic Outcomes
item.description: The article by Mark Milke critiques modern Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DEI) initiatives in Canada and the United States, arguing that they have shifted focus from individual merit to group ident...

item.title: Ukraine Offers Safe Surrender to African Citizens in Russian Forces
item.description: Over 1,000 Kenyans have been duped into joining the Russian military, according to the National Intelligence Service (NIS). Many were sent to the battlefield without proper training, leading to deaths...

item.title: Key talking points from the FA Cup quarter-finals across men’s and women’s football
item.description: This article rounds up the main talking points from a busy FA Cup weekend across both the men’s and women’s competitions. Leeds’ Ao Tanaka stood out despite his limited league opportunities, producing...

item.title: Prime Minister Inspects Damage at Simu 2000 Market After Fire
item.description: On April 6, 2026, Prime Minister Mwigulu Nchemba visited Simu 2000 small traders’ market in Ubungo, Dar es Salaam, to check the damage after a fire broke out on April 4, 2026. The fire destroyed about...

item.title: Alex Eala begins Linz Open campaign aiming for improvement on clay
item.description: Filipina tennis star Alex Eala is set to kick off her clay court season at the Linz Open in Austria, taking on local player Julia Grabher in the round of 32. The 20-year-old Eala, currently ranked No....

item.title: PDEA 9 Seizes P6.8 Million Worth of Shabu in Zamboanga, Arrests Suspect with BARMM Ties
item.description: Agents from the Philippine Drug Enforcement Agency (PDEA) 9 successfully arrested Mahadil Asah Abdul, also known as Mahang, on April 5, 2026, in Barangay Lunzuran, Zamboanga City. The arrest followed ...

item.title: Brian Masse reflects on the impact and regulation of legal sports betting in Canada
item.description: Brian Masse, former NDP MP who championed the legalization of single-event sports betting, expresses mixed feelings about the results five years after the law's passage. While he does not regret moder...

item.title: Sindh Raises Alarm Over Delayed Tarbela Works, Risks to Kharif Crop
item.description: The Sindh government has raised serious concerns regarding ongoing delays in the infrastructure work at Tarbela Dam, which is jeopardising the irrigation needs of the province ahead of the kharif crop...

item.title: African Players Shine in Europe as Semenyo Helps Manchester City Beat Liverpool 4-0
item.description: For this past weekend, Manchester City show strong form for FA Cup quarter-final as Ghanaian midfielder Antoine Semenyo score their third goal for 4-0 win against Liverpool. Since January, Semenyo don...

item.title: Kerala Techie Rescued After Four Days Stranded in Kodagu Forest without Food or Communication
item.description: A young software engineer from Kerala, identified as GS Sharanya, was rescued after surviving four challenging days in the dense forests of Kodagu district in Karnataka. Reportedly, Sharanya had gone ...

item.title: Pakistan Clarifies Position on Peace Talks Between US, Israel, and Iran
item.description: On Monday, Pakistan’s Foreign Office clarified that the peace process regarding the ongoing conflict involving Iran is still ongoing. While there have been multiple reports suggesting Pakistan may hav...

item.title: Loneliness on Campus: The Impact of Online Learning and Cost Pressures on Australian Students
item.description: The article explores the growing sense of loneliness among Australian university students, focusing on the story of nineteen-year-old Leanne Baddah, a medical sciences student at Macquarie University,...

item.title: Nairobi City Thunder ends BAL campaign with a thrilling win over RSSB Tigers
item.description: The Nairobi City Thunder ended their 2026 Basketball Africa League (BAL) Kalahari Conference campaign with a stunning 101-92 victory over RSSB Tigers in Pretoria. The Thunder bounced back from a disap...

item.title: Claude Code Leak Exposes 'Stealth Mode' and Frustration Monitoring Features
item.description: A leak of Claude Code's source code has uncovered some fascinating details about its operations. Among the more than 500,000 lines of code revealed, one of the most intriguing discoveries was the exis...

item.title: Iran missile strike injures six civilians in northern Kuwait
item.description: According to a statement from Kuwait’s health ministry, six people sustained injuries of varying severity after missiles and debris fell in a residential part of northern Kuwait on Monday. The inciden...

item.title: Important Road Safety Guidelines Every Driver Must Know
item.description: In Mwanza, traffic authorities are stressing the importance of road safety for all drivers. Mr Sunday Ibrahim, Mwanza Regional Traffic Officer, highlighted key points drivers should always consider. F...

item.title: Ombudsman cautions House Secretary General after refusal to accept SALN subpoena
item.description: Ombudsman Jesus Crispin Remulla has issued a strict warning to House Secretary General Cheloy Garafil after the House of Representatives allegedly refused to accept subpoenas for the Statement of Asse...

item.title: Philippine Government Plans Amendments to Strengthen LGU Responsibilities and Fiscal Capacity
item.description: President Ferdinand 'Bongbong' Marcos Jr. announced that the Philippine government is moving forward with plans to amend the Local Government Code. The objective of these amendments is to clarify the ...
